metadata.question: How well does IGNIS think and reason?

nodes.label: Contradiction pressure 1
nodes.summary: ops.incident-posture raises contradiction pressure through incidents.

nodes.label: Contradiction: intent-outcome
nodes.summary: 2 sessions marked intent as achieved/exceeded ↔ These sessions shipped zero improvements. Either intent criteria are too loose, or improvements aren't being tracked

metadata.resolution: Either intent criteria are too loose, or improvements aren't being tracked

nodes.label: Recommendation 2
nodes.summary: Deepen cross-pillar synergy — strengthen weak pillars, build ecosystem connections

nodes.label: Recommendation 3
nodes.summary: TRAJECTORY needs attention (D) — Improve score stability and velocity consistency

nodes.label: Recommendation 4
nodes.summary: CREATIVITY needs attention (D) — Expand scenario modeling and novel insight generation

edges.rationale: Source Breadth contributes to PERCEPTION
Whether the session/audit adapter is connected
Whether the git/commit adapter is connected
Whether a CI/CD adapter is connected
Whether a task/issue tracker adapter is connected
Event Volume contributes to PERCEPTION
Number of git commit events ingested
Number of CI/CD pipeline events ingested
Number of task/issue events ingested
Number of documentation events ingested
Signal Richness contributes to PERCEPTION
Average range (max - min) of values per signal type
Number of distinct signal names across all events
Number of distinct measurement units across all signals
Percentage of events with 2+ signals
Metadata Completeness contributes to PERCEPTION
Percentage of session events with IGNIS flags
Percentage of events with full source provenance (adapter + sourceId + sourceUrl)
Percentage of events with a label or category annotation
Percentage of events with an intent/purpose tag
Temporal Coverage contributes to PERCEPTION
Shannon entropy of events distributed across time buckets
Shannon entropy of events across hour-of-day buckets
Percentage of calendar months (within window) that contain events
How evenly events are spread across the observation window (CV of daily counts)
Data Quality contributes to PERCEPTION
Percentage of events with anomalous quality scores (lower is better)
Average freshness score across all events
Percentage of events sharing a content hash (lower is better)
Agreement between overlapping data from different adapters
Data Provenance contributes to PERCEPTION
Number of entities observed by multiple adapters
Freshness of the most recent event (exponential decay)
Percentage of events with full ingestedAt timestamp in metadata
Percentage of events with valid content hashes
Noise Filtering contributes to PERCEPTION
Rate of low-confidence events (lower is better)
Percentage of events with zero signals and low quality (lower is better)
Percentage of low-quality events that have an identifiable source adapter
Effectiveness of duplicate detection (unique hash ratio)
Coverage Gaps contributes to PERCEPTION
Uniformity of coverage across adapters (balanced event counts)
Estimated scoring impact of coverage gaps (lower impact is better)
Number of standard source types NOT connected (0 missing = 100)
Number of significant temporal gaps (>7 days) in the event stream
Perception Depth contributes to PERCEPTION
Number of signals with context objects (enriched signals)
Number of signals derived from computation rather than direct observation
Number of signals that emerged from cross-event analysis
Number of signals about signals (meta-observations)
Session Depth contributes to TRAJECTORY
Ratio of sessions marked as deep work
Percentage of sessions that were productive
Average session length in minutes, ideal ~120
Sessions per week, ideal ~2
Score Stability contributes to TRAJECTORY
Score range in recent window — smaller is more stable
How tight the score range is becoming over time
Largest single-session score drop — lower is better
Rate of convergence toward the maximum observed score
Velocity Trajectory contributes to TRAJECTORY
How strong the velocity trend is (R² × direction)
How predictable velocity is from session to session
Consistency of velocity — lower CV is better
How well velocity fits a linear trend
Intent Coherence contributes to TRAJECTORY
Consecutive sessions with intent achievement
Percentage of sessions with tracked intents
Percentage of intents that were fully achieved
Completion score weighted by intent difficulty
Predictability Index contributes to TRAJECTORY
Number of predictions that have been verified
Accuracy at different prediction horizons
Percentage of sessions with predictions made
Average prediction error — lower is better
Session Type Distribution contributes to TRAJECTORY
How well session type predicts outcome quality
Presence of meaningful session type sequences
How balanced the session type distribution is
Percentage of expected session types that are represented
Trend Decomposition contributes to TRAJECTORY
How stable the structural trend is over time
Primary frequency component of the score series
How predictable the seasonal component is
Strength of the underlying structural trend
Inflection Detection contributes to TRAJECTORY
Number of detected inflection points
Mean magnitude of inflection points
Percentage of inflections with identified causes
Frequency of inflection point occurrence
Goal Alignment contributes to TRAJECTORY
Number of explicit goals from task board items
Percentage of goals achieved (mirrors intent achievement)
How specific and measurable goals are
Rate of resolving goal dependencies
Trajectory Confidence contributes to TRAJECTORY
Number of identified growth opportunities
Strength of agreement across different prediction methods
Clarity of the prediction at different time horizons
Number of plausible alternative trajectories — fewer is better
Intent Resolution contributes to EXECUTION
Inverse of longest streak of non-achieved intents
Rate at which partial completions eventually become achieved
How often redirected intents lead to eventual achievement
Achievement rate normalized by intent difficulty
Delivery Volume contributes to EXECUTION
Total number of shipped improvements across all sessions
Highest improvement count in a single session
Inverse CV of improvement counts (lower variance = higher score)
Percentage of sessions that produced at least one improvement
Delivery Diversity contributes to EXECUTION
Number of distinct improvement group types observed
Shannon entropy of improvement group distribution
Rate at which new improvement group types appear
Balance between deep work in few groups and broad coverage
Commit Discipline contributes to EXECUTION
Total number of commits across all sessions
Inverse CV of commit intervals (regular cadence = higher score)
Percentage of commits that reference issues or tags
Quality assessment of commit messages
Test Investment contributes to EXECUTION
Most recent total number of tests in the project
Variety of test types (unit, integration, e2e, etc.)
Percentage of sessions that increased the test count
Estimated code coverage percentage
Debt Management contributes to EXECUTION
Percentage of debt items that are properly documented
Evidence of active debt tracking and monitoring
Inverse of sudden debt increases -- fewer spikes is better
Inverse of average age of open debt items
Brainstorm Conversion contributes to EXECUTION
Total brainstorm ideas that became shipped improvements
Percentage of brainstorm ideas that ship
Ratio of ideas that reach implementation stage
Quality assessment of shipped brainstorm items
Efficiency contributes to EXECUTION
Inverse CV of throughput -- consistent throughput is better
Average duration of uninterrupted productive work
Gap between current and theoretical maximum efficiency
Inverse of rework frequency -- less rework is better
Quality Assurance contributes to EXECUTION
Return on quality investments (prevention vs cure)
Inverse of defects that escape to production
Inverse of regression occurrence rate
Inverse of critical/blocking defect frequency
Execution Rhythm contributes to EXECUTION
Inverse CV of lead times -- consistent lead times are better
Percentage of planned work completed per sprint/session
How predictable delivery velocity is -- from velocity CV
Regularity of delivery cadence across sessions
Event Recency contributes to VITALITY
Exponential decay from last commit event
Exponential decay from last event of any type
Whether any activity occurred in the last 24 hours
Whether any activity occurred in the last 72 hours
Session Recency contributes to VITALITY
Trend in sessions per week over time
Consistency of session intervals - low CV = high score
Confidence in freshness signal based on data volume
Ratio of recent sessions to total sessions
Cadence Alignment contributes to VITALITY
Consecutive sessions within ideal cadence range
R-squared of intervals - how predictable is the cadence
Coefficient of variation of intervals - low = consistent cadence
How quickly cadence adjusts after disruptions
Momentum Vector contributes to VITALITY
Current velocity as percentage of historical peak
R-squared of velocity trend - sustained vs erratic momentum
Slope of IQ scores over sessions
Composite of velocity + score momentum
File Staleness contributes to VITALITY
Freshness of config files - proxy from config-related events
Freshness of build/deploy artifacts - proxy from commit and build events
Average freshness across core project files (proxy from all events)
Freshness of documentation - proxy from commit events with doc tags
Recovery Responsiveness contributes to VITALITY
Structural recovery pattern - do recoveries follow a consistent shape
Average days to recover after a gap
Longest recovery time (exponential penalty)
Ratio of successful recoveries (next interval < gap) to total gaps
Metabolic Rate contributes to VITALITY
Total events last 14 days / 14, scored via sigmoid
Trend in daily activity rate over recent weeks
Output per unit of energy - velocity per event count
How evenly energy is distributed across event types
Health Indicators contributes to VITALITY
Infrastructure and build health from commit and build event patterns
Community engagement from review and collaboration events
Score trajectory slope as health signal
Overall health trend direction
Burnout Detection contributes to VITALITY
Rate of debt increase - higher score = less debt growth
Frequency of session redirects/pivots - less = healthier
Overall burnout risk composite - combines multiple signals
Declining quality metrics as burnout indicator
Seasonal Patterns contributes to VITALITY
Optimization of work aligned with high-productivity periods
Activity concentration by time of day
Activity concentration by day of week
Quarterly activity trend from monthly event counts
Portfolio Context contributes to SYNTHESIS
Evenness of health scores across pillars
Rate of portfolio expansion from event volume trend
Degree of synergy between different event types
Diversity of project types and domains from event type variety
Pattern Propagation contributes to SYNTHESIS
Number of patterns shared across projects
Patterns that apply to multiple projects
Percentage of projects using shared patterns
Rate at which patterns are adopted - canon entries with high applicability
Canon Authority contributes to SYNTHESIS
How frequently canon entries are applied
Evenness of canon entry ages (mix of new and old)
Distribution of canon entries across tiers
Percentage of knowledge domains covered by canon
Decision Network contributes to SYNTHESIS
Rate of validated decisions from test and review events
Rate of decision pattern reuse from recurring tags
Low rate of conflicting decisions from revert/fix events
Connectivity of the decision dependency graph from shared tags
Genome Readiness contributes to SYNTHESIS
Foundational patterns identified for genome from canon entries
Practical utility based on canon applications and forge implementations
Efficiency of knowledge compression - canon per event ratio
Readiness for knowledge transfer from documented patterns and canon
Prediction Ecosystem contributes to SYNTHESIS
Total number of forge proposals generated
Percentage of forge proposals approved
Evidence of self-improvement from forge cycle
Historical accuracy of predictions from verified predictions
Knowledge Graph contributes to SYNTHESIS
Density of the knowledge graph from edge/node ratio
Short average paths - high tag overlap = short paths
Number of knowledge nodes from unique tags, types, and canon entries
Number of edges from tag co-occurrences across events
Insight Cross-Pollination contributes to SYNTHESIS
Rate of insights applied across contexts from canon applicability
Success rate of adapted insights from implementation evidence
Events with cross-domain tags indicating insight transfer
Novel insights generated from new event types and tags over time
Ecosystem Integration contributes to SYNTHESIS
Volume of external system interactions from commit and build events
Health of active integrations from recent external events
Low latency in integration from session-to-commit timing
Reliability of integrations from commit regularity
Collective Intelligence contributes to SYNTHESIS
Insights derived from multi-project analysis via cross-pillar scores
Accuracy of collective intelligence from pillar score consistency
Strength of consensus from pillar score agreement
Novel insights from collective analysis via tag emergence rate
Logical Consistency contributes to COGNITION
Stable improvement counts across sessions
Steady accumulation of canon entries over time
Stable intent achievement rate across sessions
Low rate of direction reversals in IQ score trend
Causal Reasoning contributes to COGNITION
Documented decisions in canon — reasoning captured
Commit messages explain WHY — evidence of causal reasoning
Fraction of commits with conventional type tags
Improvements proposed are actually shipped
Abstraction Ability contributes to COGNITION
Variety of pattern types detected
Range of pillar scores — breadth of abstraction
Variety of signal types across all events
Number of distinct data adapters contributing events
Learning Rate contributes to COGNITION
Rate of IQ score improvement over time
Recent IQ growth faster than historical average
Total event volume growth across sessions
Growth rate of historically weakest pillar
Decision Quality contributes to COGNITION
Number of documented decisions
Quality of commits — message length × tag presence
Forge proposals accepted ratio — decision quality proxy
Low redirect rate — good initial decisions
Assumption Tracking contributes to COGNITION
Number of predictions tracked — explicit assumptions
Tracked memories — implicit assumptions recorded
Proposals track improvement assumptions
Fraction of canon entries with descriptions
Inference Chains contributes to COGNITION
Patterns that reference other patterns — chained inference
Number of pillars with real data — broader inference base
Predictions exist and are checked — verifiable inference
Commit activity correlates with score changes
Anomaly Detection contributes to COGNITION
Detect unusual commit churn spikes
Detect when a pillar diverges from the pack
Detect unusually large or small commits
Detect gaps in event flow — missing data awareness
Meta-Reasoning contributes to COGNITION
Multiple pillars scored — aware of its own structure
Canon entries about IGNIS itself — self-referential reasoning
Forge proposals that improve IGNIS itself
Memories available for meta-reasoning
Cognitive Load contributes to COGNITION
Event count is in a manageable range — not overwhelmed
Number of pillars with non-zero scores — cognitive breadth
Commit rate is sustainable — not bursty
Events balanced across data sources — not overloaded on one
Trend Extrapolation contributes to FORESIGHT
IQ trend is upward — extrapolation is optimistic
Most pillars trending in same direction — unified forecast
R² of linear fit to IQ history — predictable trend
R² of recent 5 scores — near-term predictability
Risk Detection contributes to FORESIGHT
Number of pillars with negative trends — risk areas identified
Days since last commit — activity risk
Large pillar score spread indicates structural risk
Large unresolved forge backlog as risk signal
Opportunity Mapping contributes to FORESIGHT
Active proposals — opportunities identified
Gap between weakest pillar and average — opportunity size
Data sources not yet connected — untapped opportunities
Room for more events — data collection opportunity
Scenario Quality contributes to FORESIGHT
Number of predictions — scenario generation volume
Variety of prediction types
Variety of forge proposal categories — scenario breadth
Memories enrich scenario generation
Prediction Accuracy contributes to FORESIGHT
Enough predictions to evaluate accuracy meaningfully
Fraction of predictions that were verified correct
Low average prediction error
Event count per session is predictable
Calibration contributes to FORESIGHT
Prediction confidence levels match actual accuracy
Pillar scores align with their weights — calibrated system
Improvements proposed vs shipped — estimation calibration
Event quality scores are well-distributed
Horizon Coverage contributes to FORESIGHT
Predictions cover different time horizons
Commit events cover sufficient time range
Events in last 30 days — month-level visibility
Overall data freshness — recent horizon quality
Early Warning contributes to FORESIGHT
Declining commit frequency detected early
Single pillar dropping while others rise — early warning
Forge proposals not progressing — pipeline warning
Event quality declining over time
Predictive Value contributes to FORESIGHT
Predictions that lead to actions
Forge proposals align with predicted needs
Predictions feeding into canon entries — knowledge flow
Improvements correlate with trend analysis availability
Anticipatory Action contributes to FORESIGHT
Rate of forge proposals creation — anticipatory planning
Predictions followed by actions
Commits happen before session deadlines — anticipatory
Sessions include planning-type activities
Novelty Assessment contributes to CREATIVITY
Number of unique file paths touched — breadth of novelty
Variety of forge proposal types
Rate of new file creation in commits — novel artifacts
Variety of signal types — novel measurements
Cross-Pollination contributes to CREATIVITY

Predictions cover different domains
Events from multiple adapters in same session — cross-domain
Balanced pillar scores — cross-domain competence
Brainstorm Fertility contributes to CREATIVITY

Predictions generated — creative forecasting
Forge pipeline growing over time
Commit messages contain rich descriptions — idea capture

Variety of commit types (feat, fix, refactor, etc)
Fraction of proposals implemented — ideas to reality
Fraction of proposals accepted — viable ideas
Pattern Breaking contributes to CREATIVITY

Variation in commit frequency — non-routine patterns
Redirected intents show willingness to break plan
Sessions with rare session types — exploration
Contrarian Analysis contributes to CREATIVITY
Sessions where outcome defied the trend — contrarian success
Growth in weakest pillar — contrarian to strengths-only approach
Refactor commits vs feature commits — contrarian maintenance
Mix of commit types including non-feature work
Serendipity Detection contributes to CREATIVITY
Improvements in sessions that did not plan for them
Pillars gaining unexpectedly — serendipitous improvement
Accepted proposals that were unexpected category
Unexpected increases in event volume — new data discoveries
What-If Generation contributes to CREATIVITY
Number of predictions — what-if scenarios generated
Forge proposals as what-if explorations
Number of pillars — each is a what-if dimension
Memories provide additional what-if context
Innovation Pipeline contributes to CREATIVITY
Total items in innovation pipeline (proposals + predictions)
Innovation pipeline covers multiple domains
Proposals progressing through stages
Balance of pending vs completed proposals
Creative Confidence contributes to CREATIVITY
High forge acceptance rate — confident proposals
Deep canon + patterns indicate creative depth
Consistent upward IQ trend — growing confidence
High achievement rate — confident execution
Insight Density contributes to EXPRESSION
Commit messages contain meaningful detail
Forge proposals with descriptions — communication quality
Predictions with confidence and description — rich forecasts
Variety of signal types — rich insight coverage
Recommendation Quality contributes to EXPRESSION
Total improvements recommended — recommendation output
Consistent improvement output across sessions
Recommended improvements that were shipped
Forge proposals accepted — quality recommendations
Narrative Coherence contributes to EXPRESSION
Commit messages use consistent tagging — coherent commits
Pillars tell a consistent story — moving together
No gaps in score history — continuous narrative
Events are well-ordered temporally — coherent timeline

Commit messages are informative (length + tags)
Sessions with audit notes — documented communication
All pillars have scores — complete reporting
Recent scoring — timely communication
Explanation Depth contributes to EXPRESSION
Memories available to enrich explanations
Canon entries with substantial descriptions
Commit messages that explain context (50+ chars)
Forge proposals with detailed descriptions
Voice Consistency contributes to EXPRESSION
Consistent use of conventional commit tags
Low CV in commit message lengths — consistent voice
Sessions consistently include same data fields
Consistent event types across sessions
Warning Accuracy contributes to EXPRESSION

Weakest pillars actually improve — warnings lead to action
Forge backlog warnings lead to processing
Droughts detected and addressed
Evidence Quality contributes to EXPRESSION

Total event count — volume of evidence

Average completeness — thorough evidence

Portfolio size configured — aware of audience scale
Meta-Communication contributes to EXPRESSION

Predictions about IGNIS performance — meta-forecasting
Memories about IGNIS — meta-awareness communication
Forge proposals about IGNIS itself — meta-improvement reporting
Growth Trajectory contributes to POTENTIAL
Rate of IQ score improvement over history
Growth slope of last 5 scores — near-term trajectory
Recent growth faster than overall — accelerating
Fraction of sessions with positive score change
Capability Gaps contributes to POTENTIAL
Missing adapters — data source capability gap
Signal types not fully covered

Forge pipeline not fully utilized

Forge proposals being processed — pipeline utilization
Improvements shipped vs proposed — execution utilization

Progress within current tier — how close to tier boundary

Distance of worst pillar from 10K — maximum upside
Unlock Conditions contributes to POTENTIAL
Each new adapter unlocks more scoring — untapped adapters
Forge pipeline maturity — unlocking improvement system

New session types unlock new scoring paths
Scaling Readiness contributes to POTENTIAL
Regular session cadence established — scalable process
All pillars producing non-zero scores — system maturity
Forge pipeline active and processing — mature improvement loop
Multiple adapters + consistent events — ready to scale
Latent Patterns contributes to POTENTIAL
Potential for cross-adapter pattern discovery

Improvement groups that could reveal patterns
Event type combinations that could reveal patterns
Improvement Velocity contributes to POTENTIAL
